subject: Re: The Liminal Market — submission inquiry
snippet: Henry — thank you for your note. We'd be very interested to see the first 30 pages...
body:
Henry,

Thank you for your note about The Liminal Market. The premise — a night market between a gentrifying neighborhood and one holding on, where you trade memories and barter with ghosts — sounds exactly like the kind of work we champion at Foxhollow College Press.

We'd be very interested to see the first 30 pages along with a one-page synopsis when our open submission window opens November 6. Please email the materials as a PDF to submissions@foxhollowpress.org.

Looking forward to it.

Best,
Margaret Ellison
Acquisitions Editor, Foxhollow College Press

subject: Re: The Liminal Market — submission inquiry
snippet: Margaret, thank you so much — I'll have the first 30 pages polished well before November...
body:
Margaret,

Thank you so much — I'll have the first 30 pages polished well before November. My mentor Prof. Hargrove is reviewing pages 20–28 right now, so the timing should work perfectly.

I'll send the PDF to submissions@foxhollowpress.org as instructed. Should I include a sample of the color palette / cover sketch as well, or strictly the interior pages and synopsis?

Best,
Henry Dillard
henrydrawsthings.com | henry.dillard@voissync.ai

subject: Re: The Liminal Market — submission inquiry
snippet: A cover sketch would be welcome — nothing polished, just enough to see your visual direction...
body:
Henry,

A cover sketch would be welcome — nothing polished, just enough to see your visual direction alongside the interior pages. Looking forward to reading!

Margaret

subject: Album cover commission — Dawnstone / The Wren Brothers
snippet: Hey Henry! Got another project for you if you're free. New folk-blues duo, The Wren Brothers...
body:
Hey Henry!

Got another project for you if you're free. New folk-blues duo, The Wren Brothers, first LP coming out in February. I'm thinking something in your hand-lettered illustrative style — maybe a Southern gothic roadside vibe, neon sign, kudzu. Budget is $500 flat, same terms as last time.

Are you up for it? Rough concept sketch due mid-December, finals by December 20.

Benny Crawford
Dawnstone Labs Records

subject: Re: Album cover commission — Dawnstone / The Wren Brothers
snippet: Benny, yes — I'm in. Southern gothic roadside sounds perfect, I already have some reference...
body:
Benny,

Yes — I'm in. Southern gothic roadside sounds perfect, I already have some reference images I've been collecting for Ch. 2 backgrounds that might spark ideas. Kudzu + broken neon is a great direction.

Can you send me any reference tracks so I can feel the vibe? I'll have a rough concept sketch to you by December 13.

$500 flat, net-15 on final delivery, Stripe payout — same as last time works for me. I'll send a quick DocuSign for the brief once I have the track links.

Henry

subject: Feedback: The Liminal Market pp 20–28
snippet: Henry — I've gone through the pages twice now. Your line weight is maturing fast...
body:
Henry,

I've gone through the pages twice now. Your line weight is maturing fast — the transition between the alley scene and the market interior (pp 24–25) is the strongest sequential work you've done. A few notes:

1. Page 22, panel 3: the figure's left hand is anatomically off. The foreshortening reads ambiguously. Consider a slight redraw or an angle change.
2. Page 26: the lettering in the crowd scene gets tight — you may want to revisit balloon placement so the text doesn't compete with the background detail.
3. Emotional arc across the sequence is clear and affecting. The ghost vendor reveal lands well.

Overall: strong. Keep going. Send me pp 29–40 when they're ready.

Dennis

subject: Re: Feedback: The Liminal Market pp 20–28
snippet: Prof. Hargrove — thank you for this, the note on p22 panel 3 is exactly what I needed...
body:
Prof. Hargrove,

Thank you for this. The note on p22 panel 3 is exactly what I needed — I'll redraw it at a slightly higher angle. The balloon placement note on p26 is also right, I was trying to preserve the background detail but I can tighten the lettering instead.

I'm currently penciling pp 29–35 and hope to have them inked by end of January. I'll send them your way as soon as the inks dry.

Also: Margaret Ellison at Foxhollow confirmed she'd like to see the first 30 pages + a cover sketch when the November window opens. I wanted you to know before I finalize this batch.

Gratefully,
Henry

subject: Nashville Zine Fest 2026 — vendor table confirmation
snippet: Congratulations! Your vendor application has been accepted. Table #37, Booth Row C...
body:
Hi Henry,

Congratulations! Your vendor application for Nashville Zine Fest 2026 has been accepted.

Details:
- Date: Saturday, October 31, 2026
- Setup: Friday October 30, 5–8 PM; Saturday October 31, 8–10 AM
- Table: #37, Booth Row C (6ft table, 2 chairs)
- Location: Nashville Fairgrounds, 500 Wedgewood Ave, Nashville TN 37203

Table fee of $60 is due by October 1. Payment link: zinefest.square.site/vendor-pay

We're so excited to have you!

Nashville Zine Fest Team

subject: Re: Nashville Zine Fest 2026 — vendor table confirmation
snippet: Thank you! Table #37 noted. I'll send payment before October 1...
body:
Thank you! Table #37 in Row C is perfect. I'll send payment before October 1 via the Square link.

Quick question: is there a limit on how many zine titles we can bring? I'm planning to self-publish Chapter 1 of my graphic novel as a standalone zine specifically for this event, plus a few mini-prints.

Thanks,
Henry Dillard
@henrydrawsthings

subject: Holiday poster commission — Hillsboro Village venue
snippet: Hi Henry — we loved the work you did for us last year. We'd love to commission you again...
body:
Hi Henry,

We loved the work you did for the Harvest Show flyer last year — several patrons asked if prints were available! We'd love to commission you again for our 2026 holiday season poster. The show runs December 19–20.

Same arrangement as before: $300 flat, final digital files (print-ready PDF + web JPEG). Rough concept by October 9 and final artwork by November 20 would work perfectly for our print deadline.

Let us know if you're interested.

Thanks,
Cara Lund
Hillsboro Village Events

subject: Re: Holiday poster commission — Hillsboro Village venue
snippet: Cara — yes, I'd love to do it again. Oct 9 rough, Nov 20 final works perfectly for me...
body:
Cara,

Yes, I'd love to do it again. Oct 9 rough, Nov 20 final — that timeline works perfectly for me.

$300 flat, same terms as last year. I'll draw up a quick brief and send a DocuSign before I start sketches. Are there any specific themes or colors the venue wants to carry through this season, or should I just riff on the holiday show lineup?

Henry

subject: Thanksgiving — Murfreesboro plans
snippet: Hi sweetheart! Just wanted to confirm you're coming to Murfreesboro for Thanksgiving...
body:
Hi sweetheart!

Just wanted to confirm you're coming to Murfreesboro for Thanksgiving. I'm planning on the full spread — cornbread dressing, sweet potatoes, and your grandmother Dot's pecan pie recipe (I found it in her old recipe box).

Your uncle Frank will be there too, and he has some forms for you to sign for the tax prep.

Let me know if Derek is coming or if you need a ride from Nashville — I can ask your dad to pick you up.

Love you,
Mom

subject: Re: Thanksgiving — Murfreesboro plans
snippet: Mom — yes, I'll be there! Derek's doing his family thing so it'll just be me...
body:
Mom,

Yes, I'll be there! Derek's doing his family thing in Memphis so it'll just be me. No need for a ride — I can catch a bus or Uber.

Grandma Dot's pecan pie recipe sounds amazing, that's the best news. Also remind Uncle Frank I'll need the freelance mileage sheet too — I started logging bike commutes on Strava this year.

Love you too,
Henry

snippet: Hey — I updated the Figma mood board for Ch. 2 backgrounds. Also the studio garage door code...
body:
Hey,

I updated the Figma mood board for Ch. 2 backgrounds — added a bunch of Southern small-town architecture refs from a Lexington road trip last month. Some really good awning textures in there.

Also the studio garage door code changed (landlord thing) — new code is 7742. And I'll be in Tuesday morning around 10 if you want to sync on the print run logistics for Zine Fest.

Jake

subject: Re: Studio key + Figma share — Dec sched
snippet: Got the new code, thanks. I'll be in Tuesday around 10 too — can we talk print budget?...
body:
Got the new code, thanks. I'll be in Tuesday around 10 too — can we talk print budget for the Zine Fest zine? I'm thinking a short run of 50 saddle-stitched copies for Chapter 1. Wondering if your letterpress shop has newsprint stock for the guts and a heavier card cover.

Also added some neon sign refs to the Figma board for the Dawnstone commission — you'll see the new page.

Henry

subject: 2025 tax return — final docs
snippet: Henry — your 2025 federal and state returns are done. I've uploaded the PDFs to the shared folder...
body:
Henry,

Your 2025 federal and state returns are done. I've uploaded the signed PDFs to the Google Drive folder I shared with you ("Henry 2025 Taxes"). Federal refund of $214 should arrive within 2–3 weeks via direct deposit to Brightpath.

Reminders for 2026: keep logging your freelance income in QuickBooks and save all supply receipts (Blick, art supply stores) — those are deductible as business expenses. Your mileage if you use Derek's car for client meetings counts too.

Let me know if any questions.

Uncle Frank

snippet: No limit on titles! Bring as many as you'd like, just make sure everything fits your 6ft table...
body:
Hi Henry,

No limit on titles! Bring as many as you'd like — just make sure everything fits within your 6ft table space, leaving room for a few chairs.

And we'd love to see a graphic novel chapter zine — that's exactly the kind of original work that gets people excited.

See you in October!
Zine Fest Team

Hey Henry,

Jake's birthday is December 12, right? Are you guys doing anything? I could spot you a dinner — pick somewhere in Germantown that you two like.

Also, I know you've been working hard on the novel. Your mom says it's getting close. If you need anything — print budget, art supplies — just let me know.

Dad

subject: pages 20-24 feedback
snippet: Henry — I read through pages 20-24 last night. The mortar wall scene is doing exactly what...
body:
Henry — I read through pages 20-24 last night. The mortar wall scene is doing exactly what a threshold sequence should. One thought: consider whether the caption on p.22 is redundant with the image below it. Both are strong; together they may crowd. Talk soon.

Dennis

subject: pages 25-28 feedback
snippet: Henry — pages 25-28 arrived cleanly. The night market's interior is finally opening up. Th...
body:
Henry — pages 25-28 arrived cleanly. The night market's interior is finally opening up. The two-page spread on 26-27 is your best sustained composition yet. Small nudge: the ghost trader's dialogue in the middle tier reads too contemporary. Try older cadences.

D

subject: Foxhollow window prep
snippet: Henry — a few notes before you finalize the sample packet. Include a one-page project stat...
body:
Henry — a few notes before you finalize the sample packet. Include a one-page project statement, not a synopsis. Foxhollow prefers voice over plot summary. Attach the first 30 pages as one PDF, not a folio. And if you can, add three pages from later in the book — they want to see range.

D

subject: MICE Boston call for anthology
snippet: Henry — passing along a call I saw from the MICE Boston anthology on 'liminal spaces.' Sho...
body:
Henry — passing along a call I saw from the MICE Boston anthology on 'liminal spaces.' Short story, 8-10 pp. Deadline mid-Feb. Might be worth a shot as a warm-up piece — the theme is basically your book in miniature.

D

subject: Recommendation for teaching workshop

body:
Henry — I'm happy to write a letter for the Ashvale summer workshop application. Send me the packet requirements when you have them.

D

subject: SPX prep questions
snippet: Henry — a quick answer to your SPX questions: yes, cash box + Square reader; yes, print mo...
body:
Henry — a quick answer to your SPX questions: yes, cash box + Square reader; yes, print more mini-comics than you think; and no, you don't need a full banner your first year. Table setup matters less than being present at the table.

D

subject: Panel proposal — Southern Cartoonists Guild
snippet: Henry — the SCG is soliciting panel proposals for their spring symposium. 'Regional Voice ...
body:
Henry — the SCG is soliciting panel proposals for their spring symposium. 'Regional Voice in Sequential Art' would be a natural fit for you. I'd co-propose. Deadline Feb 20. Let me know.

D

subject: Winter mentor check-in
snippet: Henry — thinking of you as the Foxhollow deadline approaches. If the perfectionist spiral ...
body:
Henry — thinking of you as the Foxhollow deadline approaches. If the perfectionist spiral starts, remember that the version you send is the version they read. Not the imagined perfect version. Get me the packet a week before if you can and I'll read fresh.

D

subject: Chapter 3 structure question
snippet: Henry — on your chapter-3 structure question: the two-timeline idea can work but it needs ...
body:
Henry — on your chapter-3 structure question: the two-timeline idea can work but it needs a single visual anchor across both. Otherwise the reader loses their footing. Think about what recurring object appears in both timelines.

D

subject: re: The Lemire influence
snippet: Henry — glad you finally read Essex County. You'll see the negative-space technique everyw...
body:
Henry — glad you finally read Essex County. You'll see the negative-space technique everywhere in your own work now. That's fine. Influence is a starting line, not a finish line.

D

subject: Dawnstone spring 2026 EP — commission brief
snippet: Henry — kicking off the spring EP now. Same rate as before, $500 for a full cover. Vibe: i...
body:
Henry — kicking off the spring EP now. Same rate as before, $500 for a full cover. Vibe: interior domestic scenes, warm palette, human-scale. I'll send the album title once we lock it. Timeline: rough by Feb 20, final by Mar 15. Cool?

Benny

subject: re: Dawnstone spring 2026 — rough sketch
snippet: Rough looks great. The kitchen-table composition sells it. Only thought: can the mug of co...
body:
Rough looks great. The kitchen-table composition sells it. Only thought: can the mug of coffee on the counter be more central? It's the album's motif and I want it readable at thumbnail scale.

B

Benny

B

subject: Sunday call — 4:45
snippet: Just a reminder honey. Call me at 4:45 CT tomorrow. Grandma Dot might be at Frank's, if so...
body:
Just a reminder honey. Call me at 4:45 CT tomorrow. Grandma Dot might be at Frank's, if so I'll hand her the phone. Love you.

Mom

subject: Care package on the way
snippet: Sent a small box today — cornbread mix, the flannel you left here in October, and a book A...
body:
Sent a small box today — cornbread mix, the flannel you left here in October, and a book Aunt Bev thought you'd like. Should arrive Wed or Thu. Love you honey.

Mom

subject: Aunt Bev sent a link about comics
snippet: Aunt Bev sent me this article about a Nashville comics guy and I forwarded it. She always ...
body:
Aunt Bev sent me this article about a Nashville comics guy and I forwarded it. She always thinks of you when she sees anything comic-related. Love you.

Mom

subject: Thanksgiving grocery run — what do you want

body:
Sweetheart — planning the Thanksgiving grocery run. Any requests? Green bean casserole for sure. Mac and cheese for you. Anything else?

Mom

subject: Christmas — visit dates
snippet: Honey — trying to figure out Christmas week. Dad wants Christmas Day at Grandma Dot's, of ...
body:
Honey — trying to figure out Christmas week. Dad wants Christmas Day at Grandma Dot's, of course. I'd love you at my place Christmas Eve for our usual. Text me your schedule.

Mom

Honey — pretty sure you're due for your annual physical this month. Riverside Clinic. Make the appointment please. Love you.

Mom
